spec_match.rationale: The matrix now follows CSS Ruby Level 1: the normative values are auto and spaces, with none presented as a legacy alias of spaces rather than a third layout behavior.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the toggles and the live alias probe. The page reports parser support, CSSOM serialization, computed values, and measured spaces-vs-none sample widths in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: The legacy alias edge case is now exercised directly. In the current Chrome 148 verification run, ruby-overhang support is not exposed, so the probe reports not accepted/not serialized while still showing the expected spaces/none layout comparison.

references.rationale: The concept page now embeds the required ChromeStatus link, the CSS Ruby specification section, and the parent feature index link.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at /v151/css-ruby-overhang-property/locale-script-matrix/?verify=before-none-alias: the lede described all three ruby-overhang values, the notes claimed none disallowed all overhang and created gaps, the none toggle appeared as an independent mode, and the page had no ChromeStatus link. The repaired page was tested at /v151/css-ruby-overhang-property/locale-script-matrix/?verify=after-none-alias-final: the lede and notes identify none as a legacy alias of spaces, the matrix marks the none column as an alias, the live probe reports parser/CSSOM/computed/measurement evidence, the toggles still isolate auto/spaces/none alias views, ChromeStatus/spec/feature links are present, and the page had no console errors or failed requests.
